dimensions.name: Decision Traceability
dimensions.description: The ability to trace how and why AI-supported decisions were made.
reflective_prompts: Can you reconstruct the logic behind a specific AI output from six months ago?
Are human overrides of AI decisions documented with reasoning?
levels: Ad-hoc or no record of AI decision logic.
Basic logging of inputs/outputs exists but lacks context.
Consistent documentation of decision logic and human oversight.
Fully auditable trail with clear rationale for every AI-influenced decision.

dimensions.name: Human–AI Boundary Clarity
dimensions.description: Clarity on where AI responsibility ends and human accountability begins.
reflective_prompts: Is it clear who is liable if an AI-generated workflow fails?
Do staff know when they must intervene vs. trust the AI?
levels: Undefined or blurred boundaries between human and AI roles.
Informal understanding of roles with frequent ambiguity.
Explicitly defined roles and hand-off points between humans and AI.
Systemic clarity with formalised accountability for all AI-supported outcomes.

dimensions.name: Risk Awareness & Management
dimensions.description: The degree to which AI-specific risks are identified and mitigated.
reflective_prompts: Are edge cases for AI failure regularly tested?
Is there a protocol for 'turning off' or bypassing AI components?
levels: Reactive or no formal AI risk assessment.
Initial identification of risks but no systematic mitigation.
Regular risk scanning and integration into broader risk registers.
Proactive, dynamic risk management with automated alerts and fail-safes.

dimensions.name: Oversight & Accountability
dimensions.description: Structures for monitoring AI performance and ensuring responsibility.
reflective_prompts: Who reviews the overall health of the AI governance system?
Is there a dedicated role for AI governance oversight?
levels: Unclear or distributed oversight with no central accountability.
Isolated oversight of specific AI tools rather than the system.
Formal oversight roles and periodic governance reviews.
Institutionalised accountability with continuous monitoring and reporting.

dimensions.name: Policy & Governance Alignment
dimensions.description: Integration of AI practices with organisational values and legal requirements.
reflective_prompts: Does your AI usage reflect your organisation's core values?
Are external regulatory requirements mapped to internal AI policies?
levels: No formal AI policy or misalignment with core values.
Generic AI policy that is rarely referenced in practice.
Tailored policies that guide most AI-related decisions.
Deep alignment where governance is baked into the technology lifecycle.

dimensions.name: Review & Reflection Practices
dimensions.description: The habit of periodically reviewing AI performance and governance efficacy.
reflective_prompts: When was the last time we questioned if an AI tool was still necessary?
Do we hold post-mortems for AI-related errors?
levels: No structured review of AI systems or their impact.
Informal, occasional reviews often triggered by failure.
Scheduled, structured reviews of AI performance and governance.
Continuous reflection cycles that drive proactive system improvements.

dimensions.name: Institutional Learning & Renewal
dimensions.description: The ability to learn from AI interactions and evolve governance structures.
reflective_prompts: How do we capture 'lessons learned' from AI implementation?
Are governance structures updated based on new AI advancements?
levels: Static governance that does not adapt to change.
Slow, reactive updates to governance after major shifts.
Active learning where insights regularly inform governance updates.
Resilient, evolving governance that anticipates future shifts and challenges.

levels.name: Emerging
levels.description: Governance is ad-hoc, informal, and reactive.

levels.name: Developing
levels.description: Basic structures exist but are inconsistent or siloed.

levels.name: Established
levels.description: Formally defined processes and clear accountabilities are in place.

levels.name: Advanced
levels.description: Governance is integrated, proactive, and continuously improving.
